title: Questions from Quarantine: Massachusetts' COVID-19 Response and Preparedness
summary: Questions from Quarantine: Massachusetts' COVID-19 Response and Preparedness
body: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article introduces a weekly web series titled 'Questions from Quarantine,' hosted by Juliette Kayyem and Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. The series aims to provide expert advice on managing life during the COVID-19 crisis, specifically addressing public concerns about staying 'sane and sanitary.' It invites viewers to submit questions via Twitter using the hashtag #QuestionsFromQuarantine. While the title references Massachusetts' COVID-19 response, the provided body text focuses primarily on promoting the series format, its hosts, and audience engagement, without detailing specific state-level preparedness or response measures. The context is part of the Security and Global Health Project's broader content, which includes related posts on topics like H3N2 virus subclades and mRNA vaccine funding.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为“隔离问题”的每周网络系列节目，由朱丽叶·凯耶姆和玛格丽特·布尔多博士主持。该系列旨在为COVID-19危机期间的生活管理提供专家建议，特别关注如何保持“理智与卫生”的公共关切。它邀请观众通过推特使用#QuestionsFromQuarantine标签提交问题。虽然标题提及马萨诸塞州的COVID-19应对，但提供的正文主要侧重于推广该系列的形式、主持人和观众互动，并未详细说明具体的州级准备或应对措施。该内容是“安全与全球健康项目”更广泛内容的一部分，其相关帖子涉及H3N2病毒亚支和mRNA疫苗资助等主题。
